simpleEnglish: A regulated cell-death process driven by iron-dependent damage to membrane lipids.
precisionNote: No single marker proves ferroptosis. Cell death, iron dependence and lipid peroxidation should be connected with orthogonal perturbations.

simpleEnglish: A chain reaction in which radicals oxidize unsaturated lipids, especially membrane phospholipids containing PUFAs.
precisionNote: Lipid peroxidation occurs in several biological processes. Its presence alone does not identify ferroptosis.

ja: リン脂質ヒドロペルオキシド

simpleEnglish: An oxidized phospholipid carrying a hydroperoxide group; it can feed further radical-chain reactions.
precisionNote: PLOOH is a chemical class, not one molecule. Lipid species, position and analytical method matter.

ja: グルタチオンペルオキシダーゼ4

simpleEnglish: A selenium-containing enzyme that reduces phospholipid hydroperoxides and protects membranes from runaway oxidation.
precisionNote: Protein abundance is not equivalent to activity, membrane access or selenium sufficiency.

zh: 胱氨酸/谷氨酸逆向转运体
ja: シスチン/グルタミン酸交換輸送体

simpleEnglish: A transporter that imports cystine in exchange for glutamate, helping cells make glutathione.
precisionNote: Its importance varies with nutrient supply, transsulfuration and cell state.

simpleEnglish: The light-chain subunit of system xᴄ⁻ and a major controller of cystine uptake.
precisionNote: Expression is a susceptibility clue, not a standalone ferroptosis marker.

simpleEnglish: A small cellular antioxidant used by GPX4 to reduce lipid hydroperoxides.
precisionNote: Total GSH or the GSH/GSSG ratio reports redox state but does not by itself prove ferroptosis.

ja: フェロトーシス抑制タンパク質1

simpleEnglish: An NAD(P)H-dependent oxidoreductase that supports membrane antioxidant systems, including reduced coenzyme Q.
precisionNote: Its contribution is context- and compartment-dependent and should be tested genetically.

simpleEnglish: A membrane redox pair; the reduced form can trap lipid radicals and help stop chain oxidation.
precisionNote: CoQH₂ oxidizes during sampling, so extraction and redox-preserving quantification are critical.

ja: 多価不飽和脂肪酸含有リン脂質

simpleEnglish: A membrane phospholipid containing a polyunsaturated fatty-acid chain that can enter radical oxidation.
precisionNote: Total PUFA abundance is not the same as the abundance or flux of oxidized PUFA-PL species.

simpleEnglish: An enzyme that activates selected PUFAs for incorporation into lipids and often increases ferroptosis sensitivity.
precisionNote: ACSL4 dependence is common but not universal across inducers, cells and tissues.

zh: 溶血磷脂酰胆碱酰基转移酶3
ja: リゾホスファチジルコリンアシルトランスフェラーゼ3

simpleEnglish: A membrane-remodelling enzyme that helps place PUFA chains into phospholipids.
precisionNote: Changing LPCAT3 also remodels broader membrane composition, so effects need lipid-species measurements.

simpleEnglish: The exchangeable fraction of cellular iron that can participate in chemistry or be captured by probes and chelators.
precisionNote: Iron probes differ in oxidation state, compartment and reaction mechanism; signal is not total cellular iron.

ja: フェリチン選択的オートファジー

simpleEnglish: Selective delivery of ferritin to lysosomes, often through NCOA4, to release stored iron.
precisionNote: NCOA4 abundance alone does not demonstrate ferritin flux or iron release.

ja: ラジカル捕捉型抗酸化剤

simpleEnglish: A molecule that intercepts chain-carrying radicals and slows lipid autoxidation.
precisionNote: Cell rescue may reflect general radical chemistry rather than a unique ferroptosis target.

simpleEnglish: A research compound that suppresses lipid-radical chain reactions and often rescues ferroptotic death.
precisionNote: A partial Fer-1 rescue alone is not sufficient to classify a death mechanism.

simpleEnglish: A potent radical-trapping research compound widely used to suppress ferroptosis in cells and models.
precisionNote: Dose, exposure and model context must be reported; rescue still needs orthogonal support.

simpleEnglish: A covalent research inhibitor commonly used to disable GPX4 and trigger ferroptosis in susceptible cells.
precisionNote: Nominal dose does not equal target engagement, and susceptibility varies strongly by cell state.

simpleEnglish: A research compound that can limit cystine uptake through system xᴄ⁻ and induce ferroptosis in selected cells.
precisionNote: Its effects are nutrient- and context-dependent; use a chemically distinct inducer or genetics for confirmation.

ja: BODIPY 581/591 C11脂質過酸化プローブ

simpleEnglish: A membrane-localized fluorescent fatty-acid analogue whose emission shifts from red toward green after oxidation.
precisionNote: The correct name is BODIPY 581/591 C11, not “BODIPY 11”. It reports probe oxidation, not a named endogenous oxidized phospholipid and not ferroptosis by itself.

ja: レドックスリピドミクス

simpleEnglish: Mass-spectrometry workflows that identify and quantify specific oxidized lipid molecules rather than only a bulk oxidation signal.
precisionNote: Extraction oxidation, isomers, low abundance and missing standards can create false assignments or uncertain quantification.

simpleEnglish: An acidic organelle that degrades cargo and can concentrate iron released from ferritin or imported material.
precisionNote: Showing that a targeted chemical can trigger lysosomal damage does not automatically prove the lysosome is the endogenous starting site in every model.

ja: リソソーム膜透過性亢進

simpleEnglish: Loss of lysosomal membrane integrity, allowing lysosomal contents to reach the cytosol.
precisionNote: LMP may be upstream, downstream or parallel to lipid peroxidation depending on the inducer and cell state.

ja: セレノタンパク質合成

simpleEnglish: The uptake, distribution and use of selenium to make selenoproteins such as GPX4.
precisionNote: Supplementation, transport, intracellular utilization and GPX4 activity are different causal levels.

ja: ビタミンK酸化還元サイクル

simpleEnglish: A redox cycle that can generate reduced vitamin K forms with radical-trapping activity.
precisionNote: Experimental high-dose protection is not equivalent to a dietary or clinical recommendation.
